prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "屈体俯卧撑" (pike push-up) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a pike push-up: hands on the ground shoulder-width apart, feet on the ground close to the hands so that the hips are pushed up high creating an upside-down V shape (downward dog position); from this position the arms bend to lower the head down between the hands toward the ground; this targets the shoulders heavily; legs straight, hips high; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: deltoids (all three heads) and triceps. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: upper chest (clavicular pectoralis) and trapezius. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "三角肌" pointing to the deltoid (shoulders); "肱三头肌" pointing to the triceps; "胸大肌上束 / 斜方肌" pointing to the upper chest and trapezius. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
